title: CreditState
description: Score, factors, utilization, and due-date risk with freshness (FR-CORE-005; umbrella Key Entities). Provided by Module 0; the canonical SINGLE SOURCE of utilization and bands every module reasons against (Rewards FR-REW-003, Credit FR-CRD-002). Utilization is a SECONDARY GUARDRAIL input: when CreditState is ENTIRELY ABSENT a consumer MAY apply the documented healthy-band default (Constitution VI v2.2.0); when PRESENT but STALE the consumer MUST flag/withhold. Money fields are integer cents; band thresholds are decimal-string fractions in [0,1]; utilization values are decimal-string fractions normally in [0,1] (MAY exceed 1.0 only for genuinely over-limit cards). The utilization invariant is contract-test-enforced (T072), not pattern-enforced.

score.description: Credit score from the Canadian bureau (Equifax/TransUnion range 300–900); null if no bureau data connected.

bands.description: Canonical utilization band thresholds as fractions. Defaults: optimal < 0.10, healthy < 0.30, warn 0.30–0.50, hard_avoid > 0.50. User-adjustable; these defaults all modules reason against (umbrella CreditState).

warn_max.description: >= healthy_max and <= this ⇒ warn; > this ⇒ hard_avoid (never recommend a spend pushing past this). Decimal-string fraction in [0,1].

aggregate_utilization.description: Total balances / total limits across all revolving accounts, as a decimal-string fraction (computed in arbitrary precision). Normally in [0,1]; MAY exceed 1.0 only for genuinely over-limit cards (which always classify hard_avoid). A provider value that is NOT a true over-limit ratio (e.g. a percentage like '30' meant as 0.30) is a defect — the [0,1]-unless-over-limit invariant is contract-test-enforced (T072), not pattern-enforced, so '30' or '1.5'-from-a-bug is rejected by the fixture.

aggregate_band.description: Derived band for aggregate_utilization against bands.

per_card.description: Per-card utilization so consumers can evaluate both per-card and aggregate effects without recomputing.

utilization.description: balance/limit as a decimal-string fraction. Normally in [0,1]; MAY exceed 1.0 only for a genuinely over-limit card (classifies hard_avoid). The [0,1]-unless-over-limit invariant is contract-test-enforced (T072), not pattern-enforced.

due_date_risk.description: true if a statement payment is due within the at-risk window.

utilization_source.description: Provenance of utilization. assumed_healthy_default is the documented-default value a CONSUMER substitutes when CreditState is entirely absent (Constitution VI v2.2.0); the spine itself never emits assumed_healthy_default.

freshness.description: Stale CreditState ⇒ consumers flag/withhold (do NOT silently fall back to the default; the default applies only to total ABSENCE).
